reviews.review_msg: Really Enthusiastic but the lectures are very long, and questions that get graded are always "randomly" selected which is kinda anoying because why bother doing the whole lab then? Study guide is very unclear what to study and often tests/midterms focused more on one unit then evenly across them.

He really tried, and I'm sure he's a nice person but the course was really dry (lol no pun intended). I don't think he was the problem, I think it was that good-awful TA who literally deducted points for font size on a poster. Other than that, no textbook and relatively easy concepts, for some reason though, people didn't really do well.
he is so nice and try to make class a fun.
